sourceMovie.title: Moonstruck
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Moonstruck for the same reason it broke through in 1987: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Norman Jewison keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Letters to Juliet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Moonstruck clicked for you, Letters to Juliet (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Moonstruck?
faq.answer: Try Letters to Juliet, The Apartment, The Nanny Diaries—each shares DNA with Moonstruck while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Moonstruck?
faq.answer: Moonstruck (1987) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Moonstruck worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
